decisions.decision: Use StateTree exclusively. Never Behavior Tree.
decisions.rationale: StateTree offers cleaner phase separation and better editor tooling for this project's AI complexity. BT_Foreman archived as reference only.

decisions.decision: SmartObjects handle discovery and slot claiming. No custom scan/claim/release logic.
decisions.rationale: Engine-native spatial queries and slot management are more robust and less code than custom systems. SmartObject subsystem auto-registers slots and handles lifecycle.

decisions.decision: Foreman mediates all SmartObject assignments. Workers do NOT autonomously find SmartObjects.
decisions.rationale: Preserves command hierarchy. Foreman is always the coordinator. Non-standard UE5 pattern — intentional, not debt.

decisions.decision: Worker holds the SmartObject claim, not the Foreman.
decisions.rationale: Prevents Foreman from becoming a bottleneck. Foreman identifies the match and sends the claim handle. Worker holds the actual claim so failure/timeout releases automatically.

decisions.decision: All AI, controllers, and shared data types in C++. Blueprint only for animations, designer-tweakable vars, and visual debug.
decisions.rationale: AI logic must survive Blueprint graph changes and be deterministic. BP_FOREMAN_V2 is a thin shell by design.

decisions.decision: Two worker classes, not one: General Worker and AutoBot. Different physical capabilities, same IWytchCommandable contract.
decisions.rationale: Generalists and specialists have fundamentally different capability sets. Same coordination protocol, different tags. Adding a new worker type = new tag set, not new code.

decisions.decision: UForeman_BrainComponent lives on AForeman_AIController, not on the Character.
decisions.rationale: Controller persists across repossession. Placing cognitive components on the Character risks losing state on unpossess.
SmartObjects handle claiming. The interface should only define what happens during work, not who owns the slot. Claiming belongs to the subsystem.

decisions.decision: IWytchInteractable is a PROTECTED class. Always check blast radius before any modification.
decisions.rationale: Confirmed by live hierarchy (2026-03-09, 23 classes). IWytchInteractable has blast_radius_size 3 — the highest in the project. Direct dependents: IWytchCarryable, IWytchWorkSite, UForemanSurveyComponent. Transitive: anything that depends on IWytchCarryable or IWytchWorkSite (which includes all worker-object interaction code). Any signature change, added UFUNCTION, or modified return type propagates through the entire interaction layer. This is the foundational interface — it must be treated as a stable contract.
pre_modify_checklist: Re-run generate_hierarchy.py to confirm current dependents
Identify every class in blast_radius that overrides any IWytchInteractable function
Confirm change does not break IWytchWorkSite BeginWork/TickWork/EndWork contract
Confirm change does not break IWytchCarryable pickup/carry contract
Check UForemanSurveyComponent — it queries IWytchInteractable via interface cast
Explicit Ricky approval required before committing

decisions.decision: SmartObject subsystem queries are the authoritative ground truth for work availability. All tag-based world scans (GetAllActorsWithTag for workers/workstations) are dead ends and must not be added or re-added.
decisions.rationale: Tag inference is fragile — tags can fall out of sync with actual game state, are not slot-aware, and bypass the SmartObject lifecycle. USmartObjectSubsystem::FindSmartObjects() + GetSlotState() == Free is the correct query path. Confirmed working in conditions layer (SES-004: HasIdleWorkers + HasAvailableWork) and evaluator layer (ScanWorld). Applying to tasks layer next (Steps 2f+2g). DE-004 fully retired.
decisions.canonical_pattern: GetRegisteredWorkers() + IWytchCommandable::Execute_GetWorkerState() for workers; FindSmartObjects() + GetSlotState() for work
